key_risks: Hawkish FOMC dots and rising 10Y yield (4.43%) could trigger a growth/semiconductor drawdown before exit
Semiconductor and Taiwan concentration creates correlated downside if AI sentiment or July earnings disappoint
Sticky inflation (4.2% CPI, energy +23.5% YoY) could pressure multiples broadly
July CPI (7/14) and June jobs (7/2) surprises could spike volatility within the window

portfolio.rationale: Strongest momentum across all timeframes (84% 6m, 14% 30d); AI capex cycle and semiconductor supply chain remain dominant earnings driver heading into July earnings season.
Momentum factor leading broad market with persistent trend; captures rotation winners with lower single-sector concentration risk than pure semis.
Strong semiconductor-linked momentum (14.5% 30d) with TSMC earnings as a catalyst in mid-July; benefits from same AI demand theme.
Broad strength (5.8% 7d, 5.2% 30d) supported by aerospace/defense and resilient retail sales; cyclical exposure with less rate sensitivity.
Hedge against sticky inflation (CPI 4.2% YoY, hawkish dots) and rising yields; provides ballast given elevated VIX and FOMC hawkish tilt risk to equities.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward strongest momentum/AI-semiconductor themes with industrials cyclicality, partially hedged with short Treasuries against hawkish Fed and sticky inflation backdrop.
rationale_summary: Concentrate in trend-leading semiconductors, momentum, and Taiwan while balancing with industrials and short-duration cash to manage rate/inflation risk over the close-to-close month.
